The greatest sports franchise ever, The Haps, robbed already once of the Cup by New York now down 3-2. And Calgary won no games.
The weird new seeding meant some odd match-ups.
Of course, the idea behind the playoffs in any sport is to have the better teams playing the lesser. This is fair.
But the Pens, who had the second most points in the league with 111 have to play Columbus, who had 108. Unfair for both teams.
But the Pens moved on, which was what you want to see. Columbus with an absolutely unfair draw though.
Chicago makes more sense as a number one seed takes on a team with far fewer points.This qualifies as a true upset, and the sweep incredible
So, 1 out of 2 prove to be fair so far.
On to Edmonton.Not a great record, there are 6 of the 16 playoff teams better than you but you get to play a team with a worse record.1 of 3
Capitals vs. Leafs. The most fair one yet. The best record in the league vs. a team who barely clawed their way in. And what a series.
In game period one, game one, the Leafs fans can't believe it. a minute and a half in and they score. This does not happen.
And then what? halfway through period 1 an insurance goal. up 2-0 on the road against the mighty Ovechkin and his boys.
Washington scores a power play goal a few minutes later, but still, the first period ends and the Leafs lead 2-1. WOW!!!!!!
Period 2, and 4 minutes in Washington strikes again and it is Toronto 2- Justin Williams 2.
3rd period produces no score and this is very good news if you are the underdog. Next score wins is very good for the underdog.
Toronto loses, allowing a goal late in OT1, but it is the first Washington Goal allowed in over 40 minutes, that's more than 2 periods.
And while a loss is a loss is a loss, The Leafs have a lot of hope in this series.
Game 2 now and can the Leafs be competitive again? We'll see.
This start is different. Instead of an instant goal by Toronto,they incur a penalty 21 seconds into the game as Gardiner cross-checks Wilson
Yes, the same Gardiner who scored first in game 1 cross-checks the same Wilson who scored last.
The Washington fans are on their feet. They can sense early nerves. Is there blood in the frozen water. Not yet. Penalty is killed.

But just 2 minutes later and another Toronto penalty, this time  to Justin Williams. Yes,same Justin Williams that scored the insurance goal
But Toronto scores again and then, as the period is in its last minute, they score. I say they because I don't remember he.
Toronto has won the first period yet again.
Elated.
https://t.co/8c6wrvWHjx thread . nuff said.
2nd period, an early penalty leads to an early Washington goal, 1-1.
And it is the mighty Ovechkin, and that is never good for your esteem as a guy who plays on the other team.
Another Leaf penalty later in the period,another Washington score and this is the first time the Caps have led in regulation in the series
The lead doesn't last long and around mid-game point Toronto equals it.
And, then, an amazing thing happens. With only a half-minute left in the frame, Orlov blocks Bozac but in the process trips him.
15 seconds later, and 15 seconds from the horn, Toronto scores and goes to the locker room up 3-2.
If they can replicate the scoreless third period of game 1, the Leafs win, steal one on the road.
But, no, Backstrom scores in the middle of the 3rd and it is another OT.
Halfway through OT1, Washington is exerting incredible pressure on Toronto and draws them into a penalty.
Immediately Ovechkin blasts what looked like the winner, but Anderson made the save and continued to stop the Caps, killing the penalty.
And then, with only 3 minutes left in OT, Washington commits a penalty of it's own. This is Toronto's moment. Nothing happens. A horn blows.
OT number 2.
The 2nd OT is penalty free and halfway through Toronto wins it. Series is tied 1-1. Leaf fans go nuts back home. They await their heroes.
And their heroes return for game 3 in Toronto.
It's game 3 and once again something happens early. only a minute into the game and Caps are penalized as Williams holds Reilly.
Advantage Toronto? No. Because Reilly reacts and is given an offsetting penalty for unsportsmanlike conduct.
So instead of Toronto having an advantage, neither team does, right? wrong. 4 on 4 is advantageous to the better team.
https://t.co/upXrkEN0zr
And a minute later Backstrom agrees with me by finding mesh and it's 1-0 Washington, only the first time Washington has scored first.
Two minutes later, Ovechkin reminds everyone who he is and doubles the lead. 2-0 Washington, biggest lead the Caps have held in the series.
Toronto manages one and the first period ends 2-1 Washington.
Period 2.
Washington makes it 3-1 and things are looking like they were supposed to look all along.
A minute later, Toronto's meltdown begins as a scrum leads to 4 penalties, 3 of them to the Leafs. The Caps, already up two, lick chops.
But no, Toronto manages to survive it.
Then, only a minute after killing that, the Leafs are caught with too many men on the ice and are shorthanded again.
Again they survive.
And after all that, they score with 5 minutes left in the 2nd to pull within one, 3-2.
And, then, with a half a minute left, the Leafs score again. It's suddenly 3-3 and the Leafs fans cheer through the intermission.
In OT1 Washington suffers 4 penalties and no scores against but things are happening at the end of periods we've seen.
What happened was a penalty against Washington wit 16 seconds left and Toronto was able to capitalize in OT2, 10 seconds left in its PP.
Toronto leads, unlikely, 2-1 in the series. And they've all been OTs.
Game 4 now for the Leafs at home and after losing to the Caps in game 1, they can be in the catbird seat with a win here.
Game 4.
3 minutes in, Washington scores 1-0.
4 minute Mark Bozac takes a high-sticking penalty. 30 seconds later Ovechkin makes them pay. It's 2-0
A minute later, Leafs anser. Down 2-1.
Washington score 2 more and this one is not close. Caps up 4-1 after first period.
2nd period.
4 minutes in Caps are penalized, a minute later Leafs score. pull to within 2, 4-2.
And, then, again, the strange end of period goings-on. This one the oddest.
With 7 seconds left, Washington is penalized for delay of game. 4 seconds later Washington is penalized for slashing.
There is only 3 seconds left in the period so nothing happens. But for the first two minutes of the 3rd Toronto will have a 2 man advantage.
Hard not to score with a 2 man advantage. And also, the Leafs get to go talk it over for 20 minutes. Thats a lot of time to draw up a play.
Period 3.
In the first 30 seconds, Reimsdyk shoots 4 times. One misses the net the other three are saved.
There are 3 more shots on goal, and, incredibly Hotly single-handedly rejects them all and the 5 on 3 falls to the wayside.
It takes another ten minutes but , finally, Toronto scores and pulls to within one.  Have to be thinking of that missed 5 on 3 chance.
But still, one back and lots of time.
One minute passes and Washington scores.
2 back and lots of time.
And, again, things happen late in periods and Toronto scores with 27 seconds left to get to within 1.
If only something had happened at the beginning of the period, when they had the best opportunity of the series. Leafs lose 5-4.

And, finally, my beloved Haps. 8 teams made over 100 points, 8 under 100 points.
As well as being one of 8 over-100 teams, the Haps were one of 4 conference winners. So there reward? To play another over-100 team.
The Haps reward: to play a team only 2 points behind them, and ruin a match-up that should have happened much later.
Because it was unfair to the other team too, Also one of the 8 over-100 teams and they actually had to play a team with a BETTER record.
It was, of course, the BIG City Rangers against my beloved Habs. It is the first-round match-up of the ages.
Some Montreal fans will never forget 2017. The Haps were the only Canadian team to make the playoffs.
They got to the semis with the best post-season winning percentage of any of the playoff teams. Playoffs rely on goalies and Price was best.
So the Rangers ran into him early in the first game and took him out of the playoffs. They won 4-2 against a back-up.
Then they played the Kings who defeated them easier than any other team they face up to then.
Even after the lossto the Rangers, and even after the Kings won the cup, the Haps had the best win-loss percentage.
It was clear the Haps would have beaten the Rangers easily and the Kings easily too.
Without question, the Rangers stole the Cup from the Habs, then weren't good enough to win it. A dirty play unrewarded.
So this is an especially important series for Montreal to win. We already saw a similar play in game 1 of this series.
They again went into the crease after Price and they shook him up, but thank goodness Cary shook it off.
Let's look at the series so far. I have not wanted a team to win in as long as I can remember.
Game 1
Played in Montreal
rangers draw blood first halfway through the first.
second period has 2 Montreal power plays and a rangers power play and no score. 1-0 rangers after 2.

I only learned today that ESPN was a left-leaning network that spent all its time pushing this agenda. I watched, saw sports.
I was nonplussed at this news of ESPN being a left-leaning agenda machine. I watch it always, see none of this.
I go to ESPN precisely to escape the politicization of all that is beautiful. It is the one pure place left.
I read the criticism but hear no specific examples.
It is my experience that the sports fanatic, and the sportscaster, live in a universe apart.
I believe that sports fans haven allegiance but to their team, that sports anchors have no opinions but of the games and the players.
The players of the games.
Here is why I love ESPN.
Two people can argue. One is a Yankees fan. The other a Red Sox fan. They can argue into the night. And, at the end, they are happy.
Sports have the meaning we decide to give them. I am one who give sports great great meaning.
I am one who sees the greatest stories in the arenas and the fields and the roadways.
I am one who sees clarity in sports. There are rules that must be obeyed. I know this and the fan of my enemy knows it too.
A sport is like a reality show where people compete. The difference is the rules are clear and the combatants are the best there are.
In sports, we know who is best. It is the one with most points at the end of the game, the one still standing after a prizefight.
A man has numbers beside his name in sports. This is how you know how he ranks beside the ones with whom he does battle.
In sports, a winner is decided each year. Decided by who was best.
And you can have opinions. and they can be the opposite of mine. And I don't know who is right. But it doesn't matter.
ESPN is what I watch and I never hear the President spoken of, or his many enemies either, and this brings me peace.
ESPN is where I go to see greatness, pure and true. A team cannot fool you.
There is no left or right on ESPN. There is only Sports, the greatest endeavor man has found in his perilous journey. Long may ESPN prosper.
Good night.
Love.

Period 2.
In the second, Ottawa took a penalty at 8:00 minutes or so that was killed but still led to a goal.
There is a hangover period after a penalty where the shorthanded team has to get there man out of the box and situated.
It was during this time that Kreider took advantage and gave New York the lead once again.
A few minutes later and the Rangers take a penalty.A chance for Ottawa to tie but instead,NY instantly scores its second shorthanded goal.

I'm sorry, I must resume. When last it was, Ottawa had a man in the box and 8 minutes left in a penalty they HAD to kill.
Not only did the Suns kill the penalty, but a minute in, Jean Gabriel Page, he from Ottawa itself, nearly scores a shorthander. But no.
The Good News: Ottawa kills the penalty. The Bad News: Ottawa kills two valuable minutes and now there are only six of those left.
And a minute later, the game begins.
With 5 minutes left, Pageau scores his second, the team's 4th and are within one another time. Always down by 1. Always.
With a minute and change left, and Anderson a spectator, Pageau, and he from the city itself, wins a crucial face-off in the Rangers' zone.
Change burns the clock and now, only a single minute as the puck meets Pageau's stick and then the mesh behind Lundqvist.
The Roar.
A Hat Trick To Tie?
It's tough to beat Lundqvist once but 3 times?
It's tough to beat Lundqvist once but twice in the 2 minutes?
The Roar
Local boy can't believe it his own self. Look at the face.
The roar continues as Anderson quietly returns to guard the crease, with only a single minute to go.
A crew sees to some sort of issue with the ice. The roar continues.
Finally, a face off on center ice.
The last minute is played with the roar as it's background, and not a seat in the house used.
!st OT
Everyone has one eye on Pageau. He does not seem to be doing much. But he is. He is winning every face-off.
4 minutes in and Kreider blasts one that is gloved by Anderson. But then the whistle. High-sticking against the Senators.
Pageau immediately wins the face-off and the Senators look like they have killed the penalty beautifully.
But with only a few seconds left with the extra man, Nick Holden gets the only shot on goal.
Anderson handles it and the folks in our nation's capitol take a well-deserved, a well-needed, exhale.
The game has been a very tight affair, save the second period. The third, when New York was trying to hold on, Ottawa only managed 8 shots.
8 shots.
And this first OT is so tight, drum-tight.
No one is taking chances, and no one is getting quality shots on goal.
The one eye still trained on Pageau notices something though.
He is winning every single face-off. Watching Pageau tonight is like watching Dark Sanderson in the 70s. Except for those three goals.
The period fizzles away.
2nd OT
The period is only a minute old.
And now it is Derick Brassard who seems to be everywhere.
Brassard, the man who, a period earlier, held his stick too high and sat in the sin bin for two minutes along with the breath of Ottawa.
Your ambivalence about a significant relationship  reaches a c... More for Libra https://t.co/VKs1yEYwHM
The Rangers get the first shot, and Anderson holds tight for the face-off.
Brassard wins the face-off and promptly races down the ice. He is over the red line, now the blue line and fires a one-timer at Lundqvist.
The save is made but an early message is being sent by the Senators. They are willing to lose this game if it means winning it.
~Guide for journalists~ Not Radical: 32 hour work week. Radical: We should stop using calendars. Extremists: (space baby observing eternity)
A minute later, an icing call means another face-off in Ottawa territory. And who wins it. Who else, Brassard.
There's a flurry in front of the Ottawa net minder and then a blur.
Who is it with the puck sprinting down the left-wing?
It's Pageau. I thought so.
It's a two on one.
He's two for his last two shots on goal, but that was 20 minutes ago.
Pass or shoot?
Shoot.
Score.
Legend.
Natural hat trick for Jean Gabriel Pageau.
Hometown kid from over in Hull, don't you know.
